page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.mn4.small is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 1 shared vCPU and 4.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, it operates at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz with a single physical core and a single thread. The processor features 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 35 MB of L3 cache, with support for AVX, AVX-512, and AES instruction sets. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place the performance of this instance in the lower percentiles. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests both recorded a score of 1072.5, placing it in the bottom 25% and bottom 10% of tested servers respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show read-only small block cached speeds of 77.27 GB/sec, while uncached large block read and write speeds reach 8.56 GB/sec and 7.08 GB/sec. PassMark CPU and memory scores fall within the bottom 10%, with a database operations score of 483.09 and a Redis SET throughput of 1.24 million operations per second.

Given its shared CPU allocation, lack of local storage, and low baseline network bandwidth, the ecs.mn4.small is positioned as a budget-friendly option for low-intensity workloads. The resource density of 4.0 GB RAM per vCPU provides a balanced ratio for lightweight applications, but the shared CPU model introduces potential performance variability. It is suitable for basic web serving, lightweight database operations, simple text processing, and small-scale development or testing environments that do not require sustained high-performance compute or high-speed networking.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.mn4.small is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 1 shared vCPU and 4.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor at 2.5 GHz. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this KVM-hypervised instance includes no local storage or dedicated GPU, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps. Benchmark results place its compute and memory performance in the lower percentiles, with a stress-ng CPU score of 1072.5 and a PassMark CPU score of 1259.2. Due to its shared CPU allocation and low-tier performance metrics, this instance offers a cost-efficient profile for low-demand workloads. It is best utilized for lightweight tasks such as basic static web serving, simple Redis operations, and development environments where sustained high-throughput computing and high network speeds are not required.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.mn4.small is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 1 shared Intel Xeon vCPU, 4.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for low-demand utility workloads and testing.

tagline: A budget-friendly, shared-vCPU virtual machine with a balanced memory-to-core ratio for lightweight utility workloads and basic web serving.
